commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=dc464d3742aebf908c6c2f892a8366acbede5e86)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a regression test for startup overflow cron catch-up deferrals and changes `start()` to disable future cron next-run repair during its post-catch-up maintenance pass.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +81. Total +84 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current `start()` runs a second maintenance pass after `runMissedJobs`, and the PR changes that pass to disable future-slot repair for all cron jobs. I did not run tests because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for both a scoped code fix and real behavior proof, so this should stay in normal PR review rather than a ClawSweeper repair lane.

target.title: fix(cron): preserve startup overflow catch-up deferrals in start() maintenance pass
target.body:
## Summary
After the gateway is down across one or more cron slots and restarts with more overdue cron-expression jobs than `maxMissedJobsPerRestart` (default 5), the overflow jobs have their startup catch-up run silently dropped for a full schedule period. A daily `0 9 * * *` job whose catch-up was staggered to `now + 5s` is pushed back to the next natural `09:00`, roughly 23 hours later, so the missed run never happens.

## Root cause
`runMissedJobs` defers overflow jobs to a near-future staggered slot and deliberately protects those deferrals so a later maintenance pass does not advance them. The protective pass in `src/cron/service/timer.ts:1856` disables future-slot repair:

```ts
// before (timer.ts, the sibling protective pass, correct)
recomputeNextRunsForMaintenance(state, { repairFutureCronNextRunAtMs: false });
```

But `start()`'s own second maintenance pass in `src/cron/service/ops.ts:269` omitted the flag:

```ts
// before (ops.ts:269)
const changed = recomputeNextRunsForMaintenance(state, { recomputeExpired: true });
```

`repairFutureCronNextRunAtMs` defaults to `true` (`src/cron/service/jobs.ts:675`), so `shouldRepairFutureCronNextRunAtMs` (`src/cron/service/jobs.ts:197`) sees each overflow cron deferral as a stale future slot (`now < nextRunAtMs < naturalNext`, `kind: "cron"`, non-`agentTurn`) and recomputes it to its natural schedule slot, clobbering the catch-up.

## Fix
Pass the same protective flag in `start()`'s maintenance pass so it matches the sibling pass:

```ts
// after (ops.ts:269)
const changed = recomputeNextRunsForMaintenance(state, {
  recomputeExpired: true,
  repairFutureCronNextRunAtMs: false,
});
```

This still advances genuinely expired slots that already executed (the `recomputeExpired` branch is untouched); it only stops repairing the still-future overflow deferrals that `runMissedJobs` set moments earlier.

## Why this is the right boundary
The catch-up deferral and its protection are owned by the startup path. `runMissedJobs` already encodes the invariant (do not repair the future deferrals it just created); `start()` runs a second maintenance pass right after it and must honor the same invariant. The flag already exists for exactly this case, so this is the bounded change at the right boundary rather than a new code path. No sibling surfaces share this defect: `runMissedJobs` (timer.ts) already passes the flag, and regular timer-tick maintenance uses `recomputeNextRuns`/read paths that are outside the startup overflow protection window. Existing behavior for missing, expired, and already-executed slots is preserved.

## Verification
- New colocated regression test `src/cron/service.startup-overflow-clobber.test.ts` drives the real `ops.start()` with 6 overdue cron jobs under production defaults (`maxMissedJobsPerRestart` 5, stagger 5000ms).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.startup-overflow-clobber.test.ts` fails on pristine `main`, passes with this patch.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts` stays green (15 passed); the existing overflow test exercises `runMissedJobs` directly and never reached `start()`'s second pass.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/ops.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.regression.test.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` green (91 passed).
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <changed>` clean; `oxfmt --check <changed>` clean.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json` and `-p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json` both exit 0.

## Relation to existing work
PR #78272 (merged) added the future-slot repair feature and the protective pass in `timer.ts`, but did not touch `ops.ts`, leaving its second pass unprotected. PR #81731 (open) fixes a separate exact-second slot misclassification in `jobs.ts` and does not change `ops.ts:269`; the two changes do not overlap.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: overflow cron catch-up deferral survives `start()`'s maintenance pass instead of being clobbered to the next natural slot.
Real environment tested: the real `ops.start()` startup path (recover, `runMissedJobs`, second maintenance pass, arm timer) over a temp cron store with 6 overdue cron jobs at production defaults, on pristine `main` and on the patched tree with identical inputs.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.startup-overflow-clobber.test.ts`
Evidence after fix:
```
# BEFORE (pristine main f94a2506d2)
AssertionError: expected 1765702800000 to be 1765645205000
  - 1765645205000   (startNow + 5000, the staggered catch-up slot)
  + 1765702800000   (next 09:00 UTC, ~23h later, run dropped)

# AFTER (this patch, identical inputs)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
```
Observed result after fix: the daily overflow job keeps `nextRunAtMs = startNow + 5000`, so its missed run executes on the staggered catch-up tick.
What was not tested: end-to-end gateway restart against a live store and the timer actually firing the deferred run; the proof drives the startup function directly under fake timers.
